_description: Dynamic / Agent-Driven. Orgs are created and destroyed programmatically — by APIs, automation agents, CI/CD pipelines, or user actions. Short-lived or elastic org structures. Best for: AI agent platforms, CI/CD environments, sandbox/trial provisioning, IoT device fleets, gaming (match lobbies).
_examples: AI agent platform — each agent session gets a scoped org with limited permissions and TTL
CI/CD pipeline — each build gets a temporary org for isolated test environments
Gaming platform — match lobbies created on-demand, destroyed after match ends
Trial provisioning — free trial orgs auto-created on signup, upgraded or expired

service.name: My Platform
service.description: A platform with dynamic, programmatic org creation

tiers.description: Platform org — owns permissions, API keys for programmatic org management
Dynamic org instance — created and destroyed via API. May have TTL.

membership._auto_join_note: Users are programmatically added to the org instance by the platform, not invited.

lifecycle._note: Platform API creates org, adds users, and optionally sets a TTL. Cleanup removes org and all memberships.

isolation._note: Each dynamic instance is isolated. An agent in instance A cannot access instance B.

hosted_login._note: No hosted login — users are authenticated at the platform level and programmatically placed into dynamic orgs.

onboarding._note: The platform's backend creates orgs and manages membership via API. No human admin step.

service_accounts._note: Platform service account drives all org creation/destruction. Uses API key or client_credentials for m2m auth.

delegation._note: Not applicable — membership is programmatic, not human-managed.

sso._note: Not applicable — users authenticated at platform level, placed into dynamic orgs programmatically.

teams._note: Not applicable — dynamic instances are short-lived, teams add unnecessary complexity.

_org_diagram: └── Instance ghi789 (my-platform-ghi789)         [level 1 — dynamic, expired → cleaned up]
_zero_config_note: Fully programmatic. The platform API creates/destroys orgs as needed. Users inherit permissions from org membership while the org exists. When the org is destroyed, all access is revoked instantly — no cleanup scripts needed.
